title: euonymus europaeus subsp. subvelutinus
intro: Euonymus europaeus subsp. subvelutinus, commonly known as the European spindle, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Euonymus europaeus subsp. subvelutinus has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
